entry.title: CSRF Fetch anomaly during REST API implementation
entry.content:
I am implementing a 3rd party integration that uses basic auth (for now), fetches CSRF token and by using the received CSRF Token, I am making subsequent HTTP requests (REST). I am facing a peculiar issue. Let me write it simply:

  1. I fetch the CSRF token by putting the correct credentials and receive the token.
  2. I use it to make requests and decide to close the app.
  3. I reopen the app again and it prompts me to enter my credentials again (which it should)
  4. I type random (incorrect) credentials in the username and password fields and click on fetch.
  5. I still get the same token. 

Now I am not able to understand how that is possible. How does the SAP session management work? I mean if I am able to get the CSRF token regardless of my basic credentials, how is it secure? Or am I missing something in the configuration of the service in SICF? Below are the standard settings of my service that I have not changed.

My question is: How am I able to get a CSRF Token (the same one which I received when I put the correct creds in the first try) whilst putting in incorrect creds? Also, no matter what the creds, if I do any action (for example: create a HU number via the application), my original username (the one that I used as a part of my correct creds) is recorded in the SAP system (table: VEKP). 
Basically, I enter the app with incorrect creds and it seems like SAP thinks that I am the original user again (I don't know via session-cookies or something) and then allows me to make subsequent requests.


Note: I am making an axios-get request from my application to the SAP server to get the CSRF token

There are about 150 assets. 

 

Any hints highly appreciated.

 

Thanks

Hein

entry.title: Sybase IQ Restoration - Restart utility DB
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,
Could you please help validate if there will be an impact should we perform a server reboot after restoring full DB backup in a Sybase IQ DB? This requires stopping the utility DB but we will still eventually restore incremental backup files after the reboot. This will be the sequence:
 1 - Restore full DB backup files
 2 - Stop utility DB
 3 - Restart server
 4 - Start utility DB
 5 - Restore incremental backup files
 6 - Start DB

Unfortunately, this scenario cannot be tested since restoration requires more than 32 hours and we have a scheduled cutover this coming weekend. To summarize, could you please confirm if there will be an impact on item 5 (Restore incremental backup files) if we will perform item 2 (Stop utility DB)?
 
Best regards...(truncated)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sybase IQ Restoration - Restart utility DB
entry.content:
You may restart OS/utility DB between an IQ full restore and an INCR/INSF restore unless you start up an IQ database after an IQ full restore.

Please refer to KBA 3552044 which has been created for your issue.

--3552044 - SAP IQ - Can OS/utility DB restart between an IQ full restore and an INCR/INSF restore ?
https://me.sap.com/notes/3552044

entry.title: Re: MRP Date
entry.content:
Hello @VARSHESH_JOSHI 

What previous data are you talking about? I guess these are old but still open documents e.g. sales orders, deliveries, purchase orders etc. I've been seeing that too often. 

MRP has to see a full picture of the supply chain state so it reads all open documents. There is no way for MRP to know if an old open document is still valid or not. You have to close them - delete them, mark them as completed, technically close etc. That can be done fairly quickly with mass processing tools. 

Don't try to trick MRP into ignoring those documents as they are only a symptom of a disease. The root cause is lack of data ownership. Users don't feel responsible for their data in the system, they don't understand the meaning of the data and its impact on the processes, thus their ignore outdated documents. 

Closing outdated documents is easy, it is just a technical process. However building data ownership is a challenge. It requires change management and education. 

If I am right about outdated documents, you have a classic garbage in garbage out situation. You have to clean the house.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: MRP Date
entry.content: @VARSHESH_JOSHI So close that old data, before running MRP - see my answer.

entry.title: Re: input field editable when check box is ticked.

This enhancement makes it possible to modify standard behavior.

But when you change the value of a field that the standard process doesn't allow, you need to analyze the possible after-effects, e.g. when certain costs/benefits are already accounted for, will this generate inconsistencies in the database, i.e. find out where this value is replicated in subsequent “documents”, aggregation tables and reporting data.

entry.title: IO in Asset not updated in PO after user replaced the IO with a new IO number in Asset Master
entry.content:
Good day,

We create an Asset PO in SAP with the wrong Internal Order. The IO in the PO is derived from the IO maintained in the Asset Master A1. The IO field in the account assignment tab of the PO is greyed off.

We realized that the IO number is entered wrongly in the Asset Master A1 and proceed to correct it.

However, this corrected IO was not updated in the PO. We proceed to remove the Asset A1 from the PO via ME22N and enter the same asset number again, but the correct IO was not updated.

Next, we proceed to perform the following steps:

we enter another asset number - A2; the IO was updated to the IO number maintained in asset number A2
we remove Asset A2 and replaced with Asset A1. The IO was finally updated.

My question is this the standard behavior of SAP? We need to perform the above 2 steps in order to update the IO in the PO?

Is there any way to update the IO for Asset in the PO without performing the 2 steps?

The root cause is because of missing configuration or we need to do enhancement for this in order to auto update the IO for asset in the PO?

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Asset Management – Output Management using Work Packs
entry.content:
Overview:

In the realm of managing maintenance activities, the planning, execution, and assignment of tasks can be a complex process. It’s crucial to ensure that everything is systematically arranged, printed, and communicated to the maintenance technicians for smooth execution.

With this feature, the maintenance supervisor can effortlessly filter and select jobs for the planned schedule from an extensive list of available jobs. This feature allows the creation of work packs, which can include one or more job packs based on selected grouping criteria. For instance, you can group jobs into packs for each work order, or for each work center at the job level. This enables the maintenance supervisor to organise, generate, and distribute the necessary documents for planned maintenance activities with optimal efficiency.

One of the key highlights of this feature is its one-time configuration functionality. This automatically generates job packs under the work pack, assigns maintenance jobs to these job packs, and even allows automatic attachment of documents to output forms. Moreover, the work pack app provides users with the flexibility to update job packs—you can add or remove tasks from job packs or modify attachments before sending the output.

In this blog post, we’re excited to introduce you to our feature – Output Management using Work Packs. This flexible solution empowers maintenance supervisors and planners to generate the required output for efficient maintenance execution. We believe this feature will change how you manage your maintenance activities, making the process more streamlined and effective.

In the context of the app, the term "job" refers to a maintenance order operation or sub-operation which is ‘ready to be executed’ by the assigned work center or person responsible. A job is ‘ready to be executed’ when it has been dispatched for execution for orders that follow the phase model-based approach, or once the order is released when the order does not follow the phase model-based approach. 

 

Understanding Work Pack structure

A work pack is a list of job packs that the system can output together, along with the work order operations and sub-operations (jobs) belonging to the corresponding job packs. This feature allows you to include multiple output types and leverage support for print and email channels for the output.

A job pack is a group of jobs for which one output request will be created. The output request will have forms generated based on the configuration in Output Determination.

A job is a maintenance order operation or a sub-operation which can be grouped under a job pack.

Image: Work pack structure

Based on the configuration and selected grouping criteria during the creation of the work pack, a set of job packs will be created automatically. For example, if you want the job packs to be created based on the work center, select the respective grouping criterion while creating the work pack so that different job packs will be automatically created for each work center. Likewise, other criteria can be selected to group the job packs based on the requirements. This flexibility enables you to tailor job pack creation to the unique needs of your business, making the whole process more efficient and streamlined.

Prerequisites:

You need to follow the setup and configuration steps before using the Output Management feature with Work Packs. To do this, make sure that you follow the guides below, which will help you perform the relevant one-time configurations.

  • You should follow output management setup steps explained in the Output Management Setup Guide cloud / on-premise
  • Also you can refer Guided Answers decision tree cloud / on-premise

Before you can use the Manage Work Packs app, make sure that the business catalog SAP_EAM_BC_WRKPCK_MNG is assigned for the roles Maintenance Planner and Maintenance Supervisor.

 

Available configurations:

There are additional one time configuration which will allow defaulting of some of the information during the creation of work pack.

1. Define Grouping Criteria for Work Pack Types: Using the configuration, you can define the grouping criteria for creating job packs for work packs.

 

Image: Available work pack configurations

 

Most used grouping criteria are pre-delivered, new grouping criteria can be defined based on business need.

 

Image: Existing work pack grouping criteria

Once the grouping criteria are added, one or more attributes can be selected. These fields will be considered when grouping the jobs into job packs.

 

Image: Work pack grouping attributes

2. Assign Grouping Criteria to Work Pack Type: This configuration helps in assigning grouping criteria to a work pack type and determines the default behaviour during work pack creation.

  • Immediate Output: This configuration sets the default behaviour for sending the output without any further user inputs. It can be enabled for only one entry among the available configurations under "Assign Grouping Criteria to the Work Pack Type". This is not specific to individual work pack types since it does not require any further inputs from the user and sends the output with a single click. All activities to send the output will be done in the background, such as the creation of the work pack, creation of job packs, and assigning attachments.
  • Default Grouping Criteria: This will set the default grouping criteria for a work pack type during the creation of the work pack.
  • Include Operations: This configuration allows defaulting the inclusion behaviour for operations that were already included in previously created work packs.

 

Image: Assign grouping criteria to work pack type

 

Any one of the existing configuration can be selected for immediate output.

Image: Defaulting configurations for work pack type

 

  • Assign Document Types for Output & Define Output Settings for Document Types: These configurations can be used for the default assignment of a document type to application object types, including the Maintenance Job Pack object.
  • Assign Document Types for Output: Allows the user to assign one or multiple document types to an application object type or output type.
  • Define Output Settings for Document Type: Allows the user to enable the default document attachment for a specific channel type. The user can also select the checkbox if the document needs to be merged with the output form. Note: Merging can only be done if the document is a PDF file; otherwise, it will be attached as an additional document.

 

Image: Automatic document type attachment configuration steps

 

Creation of work Packs:

There are two ways to enable output management using work packs:

From the Find Maintenance Orders (F2175) app: One or multiple orders can be selected from the list of available maintenance orders. The orders need to be in the execution phase if the phase model-based order needs to be sent for output. For non-phase-based order types, all selected orders need to be in the Released status.

From the Find Maintenance Order and Operations (F2173) app: One or more operations or sub-operations can be selected from the list of operations. These operations or sub-operations can be from different orders. The operations should be Dispatched to be considered for work pack creation if the order type is phase model-based. For non-phase-based order types, selected operations should be in the Released status.

To create a work pack, select at least one maintenance order operation from the list report and choose Create Work Pack. In the dialog window, enter values for Work Pack Name, Work Pack Type, and Group By. The name of the work pack is populated by the system, but you can choose another name.

Choose the work pack type from the drop-down list of all available work pack types. Select one grouping criterion for the work pack type. Depending on the Work Pack Type selected, the relevant values are provided in the drop-down list.

Finally, select Save to schedule the background application job that creates your new work pack. You can check the status of the scheduled job in the Application Logs.

Image: Work Pack creation from different apps

 

Approaches to manage output using work pack:

There are two ways provided to achieve mass output management using work packs:

  • Detailed Flexible Approach: Allows users to take control of work packs before pushing the content to output queues. This gives the user the flexibility to view and change job packs, such as adding or removing jobs, assignment of attachments, forms, etc.
  • Simplified Approach: Pre-configuration allows for achieving simplified mass printing of maintenance orders with a single click. Simplified printing can be achieved by selecting relevant orders (in Find Maintenance Orders) or operations and sub-operations (in Find Maintenance Order and Operations) and clicking the Print action button.

The following steps are required to achieve simplified mass printing of maintenance orders:

  • Defaulting the Work Pack Type and Group By fields when creating a new work pack, based on configuration settings. Additionally, the inclusion of operations that were already part of any existing work pack can be defaulted.
  • Defaulting the attachment determination for output items related to job packs as part of the output management configuration.

 

Image: Different Approaches to Manage Output Using Work Packs from the Find Maintenance Orders App

Detailed Flexible Approach: This approach allows you to select the work pack type, grouping criteria, and the inclusion of operations that are used in other operations manually. The grouping criteria and inclusion of operations can be defaulted using the configuration "Assign Grouping Criteria to Work Pack Type," as explained above.

In general, work pack types currently do not have any impact on the creation or selection of work orders and are meant for future use cases.

Image: Creation of work pack using detailed approach

The progress of work pack creation and availability can be checked in the Application Logs link available on the list pages of the Find Maintenance Order and Find Maintenance Order and Operations apps. The log includes details of work pack and job pack creation, operation inclusion, etc.

 

Manage Work Packs app:

Once the work pack is created, it can be further detailed and managed using the Manage Work Pack app. With the Work Pack Management app, you can:

  • Search for and display created work packs. The latest work pack will be visible at the top of the default list.
  • View detailed information about selected work packs, such as type, grouping criteria, output status, created job packs, job pack details, output details, etc.
  • Select work packs, job packs, and job pack items and send them to the output immediately or prepare them for mass output.
  • Select multiple attachments, add them to a single output item, and trigger a single printout or email.

The list page allows users to see a list of all the work packs, filter them based on criteria, and take actions to send a work pack for output or retry output. The list is sorted based on the work pack's last changed timestamp.

 
Image: Manage Work Packs app – List page

The Details page can be split into two parts by clicking on a specific Job Pack. The left split displays details of the work pack and the job packs created under the work pack based on the grouping criteria. The right split shows general information about the Job Pack, jobs, and output items created based on the output determination settings. The Job section allows the user to add potential jobs based on the grouping criteria and sub-phase/status of the job. It also allows the user to delete jobs in the Job Pack.

 

Image: Manage Work Packs – Details page

Details page also has option to update the Output items like Dispatch time, Output Types, Channel, attachments etc. For updating the output items, you should put the job pack in Edit mode, make the changes and after making the changes you should click the Submit button to save the changes. Like the example in the below screen shot where adding additional attachment is shown.

 

Image: Manage Work Packs app: Details page output items view

 

Image:Managing attachment steps in Manage Work Pack app

Note: Only PDF-type documents can be attached to output items to create a single document. All other document types are considered separate documents.

The output moves through the following sequence of statuses before the required output item is realized:

  • In Preparation: This status indicates that an output is yet to be scheduled for the work pack. Any changes or updates can still be performed on the work pack.
  • To be Output: This status indicates that the work pack is in the output queue for either printing or sending as an email.
  • Partially Completed: This status indicates that the application must push a few more output items to the defined output queue.
  • Completed: The output for a business document is generated.
  • Partially Failed: This status indicates that one or more of the output items are in the Error state.

Note: There are two ways the output can be dispatched: immediately or scheduled. This setting can be maintained in the Output Determination and, if needed, can be changed for the job pack before sending the output. If the dispatch time is maintained as scheduled, as a BPC expert, you need to set up a job to send the output in the Schedule Output for Maintenance Job Packs app.

 

Scheduling Output for Maintenance Job Packs

The app also allows you to create application jobs to schedule the output for maintenance job packs. You can create a job to schedule the output for maintenance job packs, select job packs to be output based on different parameters, and schedule the output of maintenance job packs for a specific day and time, set up recurrence, or send job packs for output immediately.

Conclusion

This new work pack feature allows the maintenance supervisor to logically group and dispatch maintenance activities based on business requirements. Based on the requirement, the user can choose the needed flexibility either by creating and maintaining the work pack manually or by using a simplified approach to configure the required parameters and send the output with a single click. There are also multiple places for the user to send the output once the user decides that the information provided is sufficient.

Available configurations allow the user to default parameters, which can reduce user inputs during the creation of the work pack. More innovations will be added with every S/4HANA Cloud release, and there are interesting sets of features planned for the coming releases.

 

References:

 

  • Output Management Setup Guide cloud / on-premise
  • SAP Help Portal – Output Control cloud / on-premise
  • Guided Answer for Output Management in SAP S/4HANA cloud / on-premise
  • Implementing new output management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Private edition) and On-premise system (SAP Blog)
  • Refer to the SAP Help Portal for the app:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/b46b701cbe254121934ef970cc9947a3.html

 

Thanks for reading this blog post.  Please share your thoughts in the comments. Stay tuned!

You can find a collection of blogs on SAP S/4HANA Asset management Cloud release blogs here: The Link Collection for Asset Management

 

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA, or myself via Vikas Gupta

entry.title: Error "Internal error (this retcode should be handled by MS-layer)" After Resolving "Service sapmsPE
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing an issue while connecting to SAP on my device. Initially, I encountered the error message "service sapmsPER unknown", which I was able to resolve by manually adding the system ID in the services file located at C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\services.

However, after overcoming this, I am now facing another error: "Internal error (this retcode should be handled by MS-layer)". Despite multiple troubleshooting steps, I have not been able to resolve this new issue.

Here’s what I have done so far:

  1. Verified the message server: I am confident the message server configuration is correct because the connection works on another device without any issues.
  2. Checked the network: I successfully pinged the SAP server and used Telnet to check the connection, both of which worked without any issues.
  3. Firewall settings: I have verified that the firewall is not blocking SAP connections.

Given these steps, I am unsure where the problem lies. Has anyone encountered this issue before or can suggest additional troubleshooting steps?

Any help or insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model - Remote Function Adapter (RFA): Posting Logging Improvements
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Marija, and I’m thrilled to welcome you to my very first blog post for the SAP Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) solution. 😊

Today, I want to share some exciting updates about the Remote Function Adapter (RFA) in the SAP PaPM Standard Model. We’ve made some changes to improve performance and make things easier for you, and I’m here to walk you through what’s new and how it benefits you!


Note: For those new to RFA, it’s a powerful posting function in SAP PaPM, that allows seamless integration and execution of external functions. It enables effective communication between systems for tasks such as posting financial data or processing large datasets. For more information, visit the Remote Function Adapter documentation on the SAP Help Portal. 

OK, so let’s see what’s changed with the new RFA 🙂
The first novelty is related to…

Logging Enhancements: Where Posting Details Are Now Logged

Before:

As an experienced RFA function modeler, you already know that the application log is where you go to see the posting details. Upon completion of the run process, message logs provided a success message containing all the document numbers that were generated by the RFA function.

This, however, sometimes made it tough to match messages with specific line items when troubleshooting or analyzing data, especially when there were many posted items.

Now:

In the new version of RFA, posting details are logged in the Y-Table. Each message is mapped directly to its line item using the FS_PER_MSG_TEXT_ column in the results screen.

When the posting is completed, a message in the message logs points you to the function results screen, where you can display technical fields and then check the FS_PER_MSG_TEXT_ column for the posting results for each line item separately.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Why this is great for you:

Tracing messages and connecting them to specific data lines is now super simple. Thanks to this structured logging, on-premise systems will be able to link messages easily.

 

The second novelty has to do with postback timeouts:

Postback Timeout Improvements: What Happens When Postback Takes Too Long

Before:

When the run is triggered, the RFA function keeps running until the postback is finished. In the meantime, there is no information on the user interface about the progress. If the execution takes more than an hour, the function eventually times out and the results aren’t saved. Only then will an error message be logged and shown in application message logs.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Now:

When you run the RFA function, after 2 minutes, the system shows a warning message in the application log that the RFC executor is taking longer than expected, and the progress loading is no longer displayed on the UI.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

However, the posting continues to run in the background. To monitor progress, you can check the logs on the results screen for real-time updates. The Y-Table is updated as soon as a posting result is available, and upon each refresh of the results screen, you will be able to see the updated table (the number of rows will increase) and the posting details in the FS_PER_MSG_TEXT_ column.

Why this is great for you:

No more waiting for the function to complete the run to get the information about the progress. You'll still have all the necessary details even if there's a delay.

So, to conclude: We’ve fine-tuned the RFA to make everything faster and more user-friendly. Postbacks now complete more quickly, and the improved logging in the Y-Table streamlines tracking, enabling smoother processes and efficient handling of large data volumes. Whether you’re troubleshooting or processing data, these updates are designed to make your work easier.

Restriction ‌‌ ⚠️
The enhancements mentioned in this blog post do not apply to the following RFA types:

  • Post Journal Entries for Group Reporting
  • JavaScript
  • Python

In addition to these primary changes, we have generally improved the message logs to make them more user-friendly and informative. We have also added two new RFA function types – Finance Accounts Payable Items and Finance Accounts Receivable Items. Check out our What's New blog post for more information about them.

I hope you find these updates helpful! If you have any questions or comments, feel free to post them in the SAP Community Questions section. To read more about the SAP Profitability and Performance Management Standard Model, use the PaPMCloudSM tag in your search.

Thanks for reading my first blog post—here’s to many more! 😊

entry.title: Fixing the 'DiffExpPlan' Field Error in My Travel and Expenses V2 App (SAP Business Suite System)
entry.content:
Fixing the 'DiffExpPlan' Field Error in My Travel and Expenses V2 App (SAP Business Suite System)

Issue Identified:

During the use of the SAP Fiori app My Travel and Expenses V2 after migration from S/4HANA to Business Suite, the following error was identified:

Error Message:
- Property 'DiffExpPlan' has invalid value ''
- The issue occurred in the OData service TRV_MTE_SRV, preventing the application from functioning correctly.

Impact:
- The app was unable to render data due to the invalid value returned for the 'DiffExpPlan' property.
- This issue disrupted the user experience and hindered the functionality of travel expense management.

Root Cause:

After detailed analysis, it was determined that the root cause was linked to a known issue documented by SAP, which affected the 'DiffExpPlan' field in the TRV_MTE_SRV service.

The issue specifically involves the class /IWFND/CL_SODATA_PROCESSOR and its method METHOD /IWCOR/IF_DS_PROC_FUNC_IMPORT~EXECUTE. The error occurs because the variable lv_is_co_deployment is set to true, which is directly influenced by the attribute mv_is_co_deployment.

Understanding mv_is_co_deployment:
- This variable determines whether the current deployment is part of a co-deployment scenario.
- In such scenarios, inconsistencies might arise if the system does not properly differentiate between deployment contexts.

Solution Implemented:

The SAP Note 2946553 was identified and applied to fix the incorrect behavior of the 'DiffExpPlan' field in the OData service.

Steps Taken:

1. Apply the SAP Note:
- Access transaction SNOTE.
- Download and apply SAP Note 2946553 in the Business Suite system.

2. Clear Cache:
- Execute the following transactions to ensure changes are reflected:
  - /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP
  - /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP
  - /UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES

3. Test the OData Service:
- Use transaction /IWFND/GW_CLIENT to validate the OData service TRV_MTE_SRV and confirm the 'DiffExpPlan' field now returns correct values.

4. Test in the Fiori App:
- Conduct final tests in the My Travel and Expenses V2 app to ensure the issue is resolved.

Results Achieved:

- The error related to the 'DiffExpPlan' field has been fully resolved.
- The My Travel and Expenses V2 app is now functioning correctly, allowing users to perform their operations without interruptions.

Final Recommendations:

  1. Monitor the System:
    - Continue monitoring the OData service and app to ensure stability.

    2. Check for SAP Updates:
    - Stay informed about new SAP Notes or patches related to the TRV_MTE_SRV service or the My Travel and Expenses V2 app.

    3. Document the Process:
    - Include this resolution in the internal knowledge base for future reference.

entry.title: Emergency Covers for a Position or Talent Pool
entry.content:
Context: We recently encountered a business requirement where the customer wanted their Succession Planners to designate some nominees as backups for specific positions, regardless of their readiness levels, such as 'Ready within 1-2 years' and 'Emergency Replacement'

Based on community influence idea, SAP SuccessFactors released “Emergency Covers for a Position or Talent Pool” as a new feature in 2H 2023 release. This document explains the step-by-step procedure to achieve the above requirement.

Prerequisites

  • You're using the MDF position-based nomination method.
  • You've enabled MDF Talent Pools and defined the MDF Talent Pool Object.

Steps to enable

  1. Go to Admin Center >Nominations Setup.
  2. Select Allow labeling nominees as Emergency Cover.
  3. Save your change.

Outcome

  • When a succession planner adds or edits a nomination from a position card, they have the option to designate the nominee as an emergency cover for the position. An icon is displayed next to the readiness rating on the position card and the Succession Org Chart to indicate this status.

  • Similarly, when adding or editing a talent pool nomination, the succession planner can choose to set the nominee as an emergency cover for the talent pool.

Important Notes:

  • Emergency Cover is an optional, boolean-type field with the default value No, while Readiness is a required field that can be configured with more than two values such as Ready in 3-5 Years, Ready in 2-3 Years, Ready Now, and Emergency Replacement.
  • A nominee's emergency cover value is independent from their readiness rating. For example, an emergency cover for a position can be someone whose readiness rating is Ready in 2-3 Years or Ready Now.

Hope you enjoyed the article!!!

SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development

entry.title: Employee Business Partner in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Business Partner in SAP S/HANA

SAP Business Partner (BP) is a core functionality in SAP S/4HANA and other SAP systems that enables you to manage and integrate all information related to business partners (such as customers, suppliers, and contacts) in a single structure. It replaces the previously used separate objects, such as Customer Master and Vendor Master, consolidating this information into a more flexible and unified model.

 

 

 

Business Partner in HCM

SAP Business Partner (BP) also plays an important role in the context of HCM (Human Capital Management), especially in systems such as SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA, which integrate HR and finance data.

Using SAP Business Partner in HCM is a strategic solution for unifying employee data, simplifying integrations between HR and finance, and ensuring compliance in people management processes. It is particularly valuable in environments where multiple modules or systems are in use, providing a central point of reference for employee and related partner information.

The technical configuration of SAP Business Partner (BP) for HCM involves several steps. Below, I detail a practical guide to configure and integrate BP with the HCM module:

 

 

Business Partner Category 

  • Person: Natural person, private person.
  • Organization: Legal entity or part of a legal entity, for example, company, department in a company, club, or association.
  • Group: For example, marriage or shared apartment.

Business Partner Grouping

  • Similar to the SAP S/4HANA Finance account group for customers or for vendors
  • Mandatory for each business partner
  • Defined in Customizing
  • Used to assign number ranges (internal/external) to business partners

Business Partner Role

  • Roles define the display and the data of a business partner.
  • SAP S/4HANA provides predefined roles.
  • Customer-specific roles can also be customized.
  • The Business Data Toolset (BDT) is used to influence the GUI.

 

BP Configuration in HCM

  1. Activate BP in SAP S/4HANA:
    • Replace legacy objects (Customer/Vendor Master).
  2. Set Number Ranges:
    • Transaction: BUCG.
    • Example: "Employee BP" (Range: 100000–199999).
  3. Define Roles:
    • Transaction: BUSD.
    • Employee Role: BUP003.
  4. Link to Employee ID:
    • Synchronize with infotypes via transaction PPCI or ALE.

 

There is configuration in SPRO for BP:

SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner

 

Integration with HCM Infotypes

  • Relevant Infotypes:
    • 0001 – Organizational Assignment
    • 0002 – Personal Data
    • 0006 – Address
    • 0009 – Bank details
    • 0015 – Additional Payments

 

  • Tools:
    • HRSFEC_PAO – Synchronization with SuccessFactors
    • SALE – ALE model configuration

 

Practical Example

Creating a Business Partner (BP) for an Employee

Step 1: Create a Number Range for Employees

  • Transaction: BUCG
  • Objective: Set a specific number range for Business Partners related to employees.
  • Example:
    • Group Name: "Employee BP"
    • Number Range: 100000–199999

This ensures each employee gets a unique BP ID within this range.

Step 2: Define Roles for the Business Partner

  • Transaction: BUSD
  • Objective: Assign the appropriate role to the Business Partner (BP).
  • Role for Employees: BUP003 – Employee
  • Description: This role signifies that the BP is related to an employee.

Step 3: Create the Business Partner

  • Transaction: BP
  • Objective: Create the BP and link it to the employee record.
    • Example:
      • Name: Maria Souza
      • BP Role: BUP003 – Employee
      • Address: Rua ABC, 456, São Paulo
      • Employee ID: 12345
      • Link to Infotype: PA0001 (Organizational Assignment)
      • Number: 100001 (assigned from the range defined in Step 1)

Step 4: Synchronize Data with HCM

  • Transaction: PPCI (HCM Master Data Synchronization)
  • Objective: Link employee data to the BP and synchronize relevant infotypes such as:
    • 0001: Organizational Assignment
    • 0002: Personal Data
    • 0006: Address
    • 0009: Bank details

Step 5: Test the BP Integration

  • Objective: Ensure that data is correctly synchronized between BP and the employee record.
    • Test Case: Check if the BP record is reflected correctly in the HCM system and can be accessed in other modules like payroll (FI) or materials management (MM) when required.

Outcome:

  • Employee Maria Souza now has a unique BP record connected to her HCM data.
  • The BP is properly configured with the correct role and data synchronization.
  • The BP can now be used across other modules, ensuring consistency and integration in the SAP system.

 

Useful Tools and Transactions

Transaction

Description

BP

Create and maintain Business Partner records.

BUCG

Set up number ranges for BP.

BUSD

Manage BP roles.

PPCI

Configure infotype integration.

HRSFEC_PAO

Synchronize BP with SuccessFactors master data.

SALE

Configure ALE distribution model.

Final Considerations

  • Perform rigorous testing to ensure data consistency and integration.
  • Implement security controls to protect sensitive data.
  • Monitor audit logs to track BP record changes.

 

This blog post aims to help you quickly and concisely understand the Business Partner setup for HCM in S/4 HANA. The steps mentioned will help you understand the activities that need to be performed during the setup.

If you need more information about the new employee business partner model in S/4 HANA, please visit the following links:

 New_Employee_Business_Partner_Model_in_S4HANA.pdf

SAP Note 2578294 - FAQ - Employee to Business Partner synchronization in SAP S/4HANA OnPremise 

New Business Partner Model

Business Partner Apps

 

I hope this helps for a quick understanding of the new Employee Business Partner in SAP S/4 HANA

Best Regards

entry.title: Novedades Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones - Formulario 220
entry.content:
Hola a todos, 

Con este blog les queremos traer las novedades que tenemos sobre el formulario 220 Certificado de Ingresos y Retenciones (CIR). El propósito es tener bien informados a nuestros clientes sobre las actualizaciones de este reporte. 

Contexto

En estos últimos años la Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales (DIAN) ha venido solicitando información más detallada en el reporte CIR. Estas informaciones se refieren a algunos pagos o contribuciones específicos. A continuación, explicaremos cuales han sido los cambios en los últimos años.

 

Requerimientos

Ahorro programado para la construcción:

En el año 2023 (año gravable 2022) se incluyó en el reporte CIR los ahorros programados de Ahorro Voluntario Contractual (AVC). Este ahorro voluntario se refiere a los empleados que tienen cuenta con el Fondo Nacional del Ahorro (FNA). Para el año 2023 esta información se debía presentar en la misma línea con los aportes de las cuentas de Ahorro para el Fomento de la Construcción (AFC). Para el año 2024 (año gravable 2023) la DIAN determino que esta información debería ir separada de la siguiente manera: 

  • En la línea 57 se deben reportar los “Aportes a cuentas AFC”, correspondientes a todos los aportes AFC excepto FNA. 
  • En la línea 58 se deben reportar “Aportes a cuentas AVC”, correspondientes a las deducciones que se haya realizado al trabajador para el FNA. 

Cesantías:

Antes del año 2024 los pagos por cesantías se presentaban en una misma línea para empleados con relación laboral Régimen Tradicional y con relación laboral Ley50, y las consignaciones a fondos de cesantías se presentaban en otra línea diferente. La DIAN para el año 2024 (año gravable 2023) realizo un cambio. Para este año las cesantías se deben presentar de la siguiente manera: 

  • En la línea 47, se deben reportar los pagos por “Auxilio de cesantía e intereses efectivamente pagados al empleado”. 
  • En la línea 48, se deben reportar “Auxilio de cesantía reconocido a trabajadores del régimen tradicional del CST, contenido en el Capítulo VII, Título VIII Parte Primera”. 
  • En la línea 49, se deben reportar “Auxilio de cesantía consignado al fondo de cesantías”. 

Bonos de alimentación: 

Otro cambio incluido en el año 2024 se refiere a los pagos por alimentación y pagos realizados con bonos electrónicos o de papel, que también deben ser reportados de forma separada de la siguiente manera:

  • En la línea 37 se deben reportar “Pagos realizados con bonos electrónicos o de papel de servicio, cheques, tarjetas, vales, etc.“, los bonos de alimentación que no exceden las 41 UVT y donde el salario mensual del empleado sea superior a 310 UVT.
  • En la línea 38 se deben reportar “Valor del exceso de los pagos por alimentación mayores a 41 UVT, art. 3871 E.T.:” los bonos de alimentación donde el salario mensual del empleado no supere las 310 UVT y el bono de alimentación sea superior a 41 UVT, este exceso constituye ingreso tributario del trabajador, sometido a retención en la fuente por ingresos laborales.

 

Solución

El desafío que tuvimos con estos nuevos requerimientos de la DIAN, es que esta información solicitada no existe de forma natural en la nómina, es decir, no existen CC-nóminas específicas dentro de la nómina para ese fin. A continuación, mencionamos la solución que hemos entregado para cada uno de los requerimientos:

Aportes a cuentas AVC:

Con el fin de cubrir esta solicitud, creamos un nuevo aporte voluntario dentro de la nómina. Este nuevo aporte voluntario es exclusivamente usado para cuentas AVC. De esta manera ya se tienen desde la nómina, CC-nóminas independientes donde cada cliente puede parametrizarlas de acuerdo con su interpretación. Si desea más información la siguiente nota SAP contiene la solución:
 3457085 - [CO] New voluntary contribution to Contractual Voluntary Savings accounts .


Cesantías:

Para cubrir esta solicitud decidimos crear nuevas CC-nóminas las cuales son generadas en el cálculo de la nómina, y se crean en el momento que el empleado tiene pago de anticipos de cesantías o de intereses de cesantías. Estas nuevas CC-nóminas son informativas y son generadas exclusivamente para ser configuradas en el reporte CIR. Con esta solución es más fácil y flexible la configuración de cesantías para el reporte CIR. Si desea más información la siguiente nota SAP contiene esta solución:
3464714 – [CO] Severance – New wages types for CIR report .

Bonos de Alimentación:

La solución que encontramos para los bonos de alimentación fue la misma que usamos para las cesantías, es decir: crear CC-nóminas desde la nómina para cubrir esta necesidad. Estas nuevas CC-nóminas serán informativas, es decir, no tendrán una incidencia directa en el cálculo del neto del empleado y serán exclusivas para que sean configuradas en el reporte CIR, de esta manera cada cliente podrá configurar el reporte CIR de acuerdo con sus interpretaciones. 

Si desea más información las siguientes notas SAP contienen esta solución: 
3459605 - [CO] TAX - Calculating new wage types for food voucher. 
3537740 - [CO] TAX - New wage type for food voucher higher than the maximum deducible value. 

 

Pasos a seguir

Es muy importante que nuestros clientes tengan implementadas estas notas a más tardar a finales de diciembre de 2024, de esta manera las nuevas CC-nominas se generaran desde el primer periodo de enero del 2025, y serán almacenadas en los resultados de nómina, con esto, el reporte CIR del año gravable 2025 (presentado en el 2026) se generara con todos los valores leídos desde los resultados de la nómina. 

En cuanto a la información del año gravable 2024 que se presentara en el 2025, el reporte CIR simulará estas nuevas CC-nominas durante la ejecución del reporte y los incluirá en el formulario de acuerdo con la configuración que el cliente haya hecho en los acumuladores respectivos. Es importante tener en cuenta, para que el reporte simule estas nuevas CC-nóminas, estas deberán existir, es decir, las notas mencionadas anteriormente deben estar implementadas en el sistema del cliente. 

 

Gracias por formar parte de esta comunidad.

 

Omar Granados Mateus 
Tech Lead para America Latina, SAP Globalization

entry.title: Re: Link between Subscription parameters amount for recur...
entry.content:
Hi,
the approach with two charges seems to be the right one. 
However, as charges are rated independently of each other, the subscription parameter representing the quantity of the recurring charge is not available in the pricing of the usage-based charge.

So the first problem is to get the number of trucks into the pricing call to rate API Calls. This is probably only possible by adding a product configuration field in the subscription and store the number of trucks redundant in this field. But with this, you can use the pricing field mapping to use it in the pricing tree which calculates price for API Calls.

Second problem is the varying number of trucks which relates to the number of included API calls.
The idea would be to relate the number of API Calls to the number of trucks, maybe by a simple division (average calls per truck)?
2 Trucks, 700 API calls -> in average 350 calls
5 Trucks, 2500 API call -> in average 500 calls
That should allow you to keep the ranges stable. 

Best
Carsten

entry.title: Re: How to Customize scan QR Code in WM?
entry.content:
Hello @mauriciocastiblanco 

Standard LE-WM RF screens are designed in such a way that each verification field has to be scanned individually. So you would need to scan the same QR code three times to fill three verification fields from your screenshot.

However, you can replace a standard RF screen with your own - SAP Help User Exit Screens. Then you can add a custom field, scan QR code there, analyze the code content and copy relevant information to standard verification fields. It is a task for a developer, but not very complicated and fully supported by LE-WM RF architecture.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Extending SAPUI5 apps: Adaptation projects vs. Extension projects
entry.content:
Hi @Georgi 

Re-using as much SAP logic as possible is recommended for many reasons, last but not least for your efficiency:

  1. Leveraging a standard SAP app (incl. its business logic) is usually preferred, then add extensions as needed with e.g. with an adaptation project. (Note there are more capabilities in SAPUI5 flexibility, not just adaptation projects.)
  2. If your requirement to too far from a standard app, you might decide for an own, custom app. For this again you should leverage as much SAP logic as possible, in this case from the SAP Fiori elements framework, i.e. annotation-driven UIs:
    1. Use a full-page SAP Fiori elements pattern (e.g. list report / object page) and add extensions to views/controllers
    2. Use the Flexible Programming model to even more freely mix freestyle SAPUI5 with 'smart building blocks' from SAP Fiori elements.
    3. Use pure freestyle SAPUI5, in case you cannot reuse anything from the Flexible Programming Model.

entry.title: Set up of Accrual condition type REA1 in Standard pricing procedure RVAA01
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

As part of settlement management, we need to maintain accrual condition type REA1 in the
sales pricing procedure RVA01. This condition type is also maintained as data records in the
condition contract(WCOCO) with the respective percentage.

Can someone please explain the details to add the accrual condition type REA1 in the sales standard pricing procedure RVAA01 using transaction code V/08.

What to fill in columns from-To, sub-total, any routines and account/accrual keys for accrual condition type REA1?

Thank you.

entry.title: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content:
In November 2024, AWS updated its guidance for SAP Data Extraction with a Glue connector.

This adds to the previous guidance using Amazon AppFlow service. But when to use what? lets dive in

 

 

 

Amazon Appflow Approach

AppFlow is the AWS pre-configured integration service, if you are familiar with SAP Integration Suite, Fivetran, Airbyte or similars, you already know the drill. One tool to connect all, highly transactional, easy to configure.

AppFlow has a pre-configured SAP OData Connector, so we don't need to build it. Since its launch, it has been super easy to set up a data flow from SAP to AWS in just a few clicks, great for prototyping and relative small data sets replication.

 

AppFlow makes it simple, in 4 steps you define the flow, you prepare the data, add transformations, partitioning and aggregation if necessary and in a few clicks you have your raw data on AWS.

 

Integration is quite flexible in a low-code no-code fashion and allows modern file formats, the next step, depending of the case, would be to curate the data, with services like Glue.

 

New AWS Glue Approach

Now, AWS has introduced an AWS Glue OData connector for SAP, contrary to an Integration service, Glue provides a serverless data processing (ETL) service. It's based on Spark.

Glue is more suitable for extensive data integration as well as distributed data processing capabilities, complex transformations, and analytics.

So this week, AWS announced the Odata Connector on Glue. Great because we dont need to learn Scala to build our own connector.

 

 

AWS provides more than 40 predefined connectors, plus hundreds of more connectors available through the AWS Marketplace. Glue capabilities are immense, they not only provide extensive ETL capabilities, but most importantly, they store the Data Catalog, a critical feature for search and query using Amazon Athena or Amazon Redshift.

AWS Glue ETL Workflow. Courtesy of AWS

 For entire details of the new Glue connector to SAP, read this blog;

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/big-data/scaling-rise-with-sap-data-and-aws-glue/

 Key Differences and when to use

Literature

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/connecting-to-data-sap-odata.html

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/big-data/scaling-rise-with-sap-data-and-aws-glue/

entry.title: Re: How to avoid "Too many members created" message. Adva...
entry.content:
Hi @timsmyth,

if you know that a dimension is always equal unassigned, then you can add this dimension to your memberset definition:

 

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/StartPeriod] TO [d/Version].[p/EndPeriod]
MEMBERSET [d/Audit] = "MANUAL"
MEMBERSET [d/Dimension_1] = "#"
MEMBERSET [d/Dimension_2] = "#"
MEMBERSET [d/Dimension_3] = "#"

 

 

or you need to specify the [d/Dimension] = "#" on both sides of your formula.
Maybe Advanced Formulas – How they work helps you in solving your issue.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM / Memory Consumption Tuning for Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Good Morning,

I started to use Cloud ALM two months ago.
There are now 10 on premise systems (ECC & S4) connected.

The use cases activated in each managed systems are:
1. Exception Monitoring
2. Health Monitoring
3. Performance Monitoring

In my Sap Cloud ALM Memory increases regularly:

This memory increase is due to mostly to Health Monitoring:

Basically each ECC is eating 400 MB and S4 200 MB of Health Monitoring Memory in my Tenant.
These consumption is after using the default parameters provided by SAP.

Do you have similar memory consumption for your On Premise Systems ?

As, I would like to avoid paying for an extra tenant I decided to reduce the CALM  the memory consumption.
In order to reduce this consumption, I have changed the retention for Health Monitoring:
Raw Data 30 -> 7 Days
Hourly Data 60 -> 15 Days
Daily Data 120 -> 30 Days

I did this change yesterday at 14h.
Now, at 10h there is no Memory Cleaning performed in ALM.
How long should I wait before the cleaning of the Health Monitoring would be
performed?

Have a nice day.

entry.title: ASA 17.0.10: Question about calling external web services and passing binary data to them
entry.content:
Hi,

There is some external web service. I need to call it and pass binary data to it.
For this purpose I have created my own procedure:

CREATE PROCEDURE "dba"."WebTest2Run"( in "data" long binary,in "cAccessToken" long varchar,in "nCustomerID" integer,in "filename" char(20) ) 
result( "cAttribute" long varchar,"cValue" long varchar,"npp" integer ) 
url 'http://OtherSite/WebTest1'
type 'HTTP:POST:application/pdf'
header 'Authorization: !cAccessToken; \x0AContent-Disposition: form-data; name="nCustomerID" !nCustomerID; \x0AContent-Disposition: form-data; name="files"; filename="!filename"'

I call it like this:

 select * from dba.WebTest2Run(MyBinaryData, 'MyToken', 47, 'test.pdf')
 where cAttribute = 'Body'

 I need "Content-Disposition" to be specified twice in the header.

Content-Disposition: form-data; name="nCustomerID" 47 
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="files"; filename="test.pdf"

But for some reason the second "Content-Disposition" setting overrides the first one, i.e. only the second setting remains in the header:

Content-Disposition = form-data; name="files"; filename="test.pdf"

 Is there any way to fix this ?

And another question: When calling an external web service and passing binary data to it, is it possible to make http-header as in the picture below ?

In particular I am interested in the "boundary" and "muiltipart" parts of the http header ?

entry.title: FSM scheduled business-rule to send reminder e-mail to more than one recipients
entry.content:
Hi!
I've successfully created a business rule which is scheduled to run every day and to send out a reminder e-mail to certain receivers. 

Those receivers do get individual texts in the mail's body so I cannot add all receivers into the appropriate CC, BCC etc. fields.

To select the necessary activities, I'm using this custom CoreSQL query below. For testing purposes, I'm limiting the result to two activities only, see WHERE statement.

Hoping that this will limit the amount of sent out emails to two.

SELECT activity FROM Activity activity INNER JOIN ServiceCall sc ON sc = activity .object.objectId AND activity.object.objectType = 'SERVICECALL' WHERE activity.executionStage = 'DISPATCHING' AND (activity.udf.AcCustomerEmail IS NOT NULL ) AND ( datediff(day, COALESCE(activity.udf.ACReminderDueTo, activity.createDateTime ), Now()) > 90 ) AND ( sc.udf.serviceCallObjectEmail IS NOT NULL OR sc.udf.serviceCallCustomerEmail IS NOT NULL ) AND activity.executionStage = 'DISPATCHING' AND sc.problemTypeCode = 'AUM' AND sc.typeCode = 'Z_DK' and ( activity.id = '04CFA494E159483C9E651A812F0592E8' or activity.id = '44D5B71619764B1981AE8E7E57B628AC' )

I expected to get two activities as a result and would liket to send an email to the receivers those emails can be found in an UDF in the service call.

As mentioned before, validating or executing works, but only for the one activity I must specify while validating/executing the rule.

OTH, when the business-rule gets executed a certain point of time. I still get only one email and not two. I guess it's due to the execution count property of the email action as below.

I did not found any help regarding on how to send out bulk emails if the result set of a query returns more than one record.

Any hint appreciated!

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Tuesday, February 4th in Strasbourg, France 🇫🇷 from 9:00 to 15:00 CET (GMT+01:00). The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is the Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen.

The event is being hosted at:

Nagarro France 

8a Rue Icare, 67960 Entzheim

The event is kindly being hosted by Nagarro, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Unlocking New Possibilities with “Default Logic” in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving world of digital finance and analytics, the introduction of new functionalities can significantly enhance the capabilities of existing tools. One such development is the implementation of "Default Logic" in SAP Analytics Cloud, utilising the new table event called "on after data entry process." Many thanks for my colleague Christopher Bavington that pointed out this functionality's transformative approach to data management and calculation within SAP Analytics Cloud, providing enhanced efficiency and precision.

Understanding Default Logic

Default logic is a concept that originated from Business Planning and Consolidation (BPC), where it allowed the execution of pre-defined code after data was entered into a model. This ensured that changes could be automatically tracked and calculations executed based on the newly inputted data, offering a streamlined way to maintain data integrity and consistency across different models within the organisation.

Addressing SAC's Limitations

Previously, SAP Analytics Cloud lacked native capabilities matching the default logic of BPC. As a result, users often had to resort to labor-intensive processes to manage changes. A common workaround involved scheduling data actions to run periodically for all cost centers, leading to unnecessary load and inefficiencies within planning implementations.

New Capabilities with On After Data Entry Process

The new scripting event, "on after data entry process," provides a solution by enabling SAP Analytics Cloud to track data changes, regardless of whether data is entered manually, via mass entry, or through copy-pasting. This event triggers only when actual changes are made, ensuring that recalculations are necessary only for affected data points, thereby reducing redundant calculations.

Practical Application

This feature tracks changes by Cost Centers (or any designated dimension) and stores this information using a centralised array. After making numerous changes to a Forecast, the user is able to execute a data action for only the changed dimensions by using the centralised array as a parameter. This process greatly reduces calculation times, enables models to be synchronised in real time and reduces the load on the computer memory and processors.  

Simple Scripting Enhancements

Leveraging simple scripting within the optimised design experience of SAP Analytics Cloud, users can now create seamless workflows that integrate this new event with existing data models. By efficiently managing array variables and executing data actions based on changes, organisations can significantly enhance their data management practices.

Looking Ahead

Slated for release in Q1 2025 for quarterly tenants, this functionality is expected to revolutionise the way organisations handle data changes within SAP Analytics Cloud. Users keen on harnessing this new event should familiarise themselves with scripting fundamentals to maximise its potential. Be sure to browse the community for more great examples! In summary, this new table event in SAP Analytics Cloud marks a noteworthy advancement in digital finance and analytics, offering users a more efficient, reliable, and streamlined approach to managing and processing data changes.

entry.title: Re: sap cap xs-security grant authorities for various app...
entry.content:
To resolve the 401 Unauthorized error when granting multiple apps authority in SAP CAP, the UAA service must be bound to both the app and the CAP service, as both need proper security configuration.
Please double-check that the scope and client IDs in xs-security.json match exactly, including case sensitivity and use cf oauth-token to ensure the token includes the correct scope and audience for all apps. If it works with one app, add apps one by one to isolate the issue. If the problem persists, review the UAA configuration and ensure it’s consistent for both applications.

entry.title: Subscribe multitenant app in different global account
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we have a multitenant application hosted on SAP BTP which uses resources such as destination service and cloud connector. This works fine when our customers' subscribing subaccounts reside in the same global account that also keeps the subaccount that provides the application. I know this should be the preferred way to realize multitenant subscriptions. However, some customers would like to subscribe the app from within their own global account, i.e. a subaccount should subscribe to another subaccount in a different global account.

As described in this article service broker could be an option to do this but it comes with disadvantages (cloud connector/destinations cannot be used).

Is there another approach to subscribe to a multitenant app/subaccount in a different global account?

Many thanks in advance and kind regards,

Jane
